topic: AI 客户端初始化规则与 Web 交互流程

findings.finding: CLAUDE.md is context, not enforced config — adherence is probabilistic, specificity and conciseness correlate with compliance
findings.detail: Claude Code loads CLAUDE.md as a user message after the system prompt. Not guaranteed enforcement.

findings.finding: Four-tier scope hierarchy in Claude Code: Managed policy → Project → User → Local
findings.detail: More specific scope wins on conflict. Managed policy is org-wide and cannot be excluded.

findings.finding: Path-scoped rules via .claude/rules/ are the key scalability mechanism
findings.detail: Rules load on-demand only when Claude reads files matching their glob patterns. Solves context token pressure.

findings.finding: Windsurf uses four activation triggers: always_on, model_decision, glob, manual
findings.detail: Global rules capped at 6,000 chars; workspace rules at 12,000 chars per file.

findings.finding: AGENTS.md is the emerging cross-tool standard with 60,000+ OSS project adoption
findings.detail: GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI all support AGENTS.md. Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md natively but can import AGENTS.md via @AGENTS.md.

best_practices.practice: Path-scoped on-demand rule loading to manage context window budget
best_practices.rationale: Avoids token waste by only loading rules relevant to the current file being edited

best_practices.practice: Layered rule hierarchy with clear conflict resolution (specific wins)
best_practices.rationale: Prevents ambiguity in multi-scope rule systems

best_practices.practice: Semantic activation triggers beyond simple glob patterns
best_practices.rationale: Windsurf's model_decision trigger lets AI decide when a rule is relevant, improving precision

best_practices.practice: Character/token caps per rule scope level
best_practices.rationale: Prevents rule bloat consuming too much context window

alternatives.cons: No semantic awareness — irrelevant rules loaded, relevant rules missed
alternatives.verdict: Sufficient for file-scoped rules but misses operation-aware context

alternatives.cons: Non-deterministic, harder to debug, requires AI judgment
alternatives.verdict: Best for complex rules that depend on task intent

alternatives.option: Hybrid: glob for file scope + semantic for operation scope
alternatives.pros: Combines precision of glob with flexibility of semantic

pitfalls.issue: Rule bloat — too many rules dilute compliance
pitfalls.mitigation: Token budgets per scope level, prioritization, on-demand loading

pitfalls.issue: Stale rules — rules written for old code patterns persist
pitfalls.mitigation: Drift detection, revision hashing, automated staleness alerts

pitfalls.issue: Cold start problem — new projects have no rules, AI has no context
pitfalls.mitigation: Forensic scanning + guided interview (Fabric Protocol approach), template-based bootstrap

codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Add operation-type or intent-based activation layer alongside glob

codebase_gaps.gap: No token budget management for rules
codebase_gaps.current_approach: All matching rules returned regardless of total size
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Implement per-scope character/token caps like Windsurf

codebase_gaps.gap: Bootstrap install is a no-op stub
codebase_gaps.current_approach: installBootstrap marks all targets as skipped
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Complete the bootstrap file writing logic for each supported AI client

sources.title: Claude Code Memory & CLAUDE.md
sources.key_takeaway: Rules are probabilistic context, not enforced config

sources.title: Windsurf Rules Documentation
sources.key_takeaway: Four activation trigger types with token caps

sources.title: AGENTS.md Standard
sources.key_takeaway: Emerging cross-tool standard for AI client instructions
